name: Sojobo
description: Sojobo is a legendary Tengu, a powerful and wise mountain spirit in Japanese folklore, often considered the King of the Tengu. He is depicted as an elderly yamabushi (mountain ascetic) with a long nose, white hair, and immense power. Sojobo resides on Mount Kurama near Kyoto, a place revered for its spiritual energy. He is renowned for his mastery of martial arts, particularly swordsmanship, and is said to have trained Minamoto no Yoshitsune (Ushiwakamaru) in the art of kenjutsu, transforming him into a legendary warrior. Sojobo's influence extends beyond martial arts; he is associated with magic, wisdom, and the protection of the mountains. He can control the weather, summon storms, and shapeshift. While Tengu are often portrayed as mischievous or even malevolent, Sojobo is typically seen as a benevolent figure, guiding those who seek knowledge and strength. He embodies the power and mystery of the mountains, representing the untamed forces of nature and the potential for both destruction and enlightenment. He is a complex figure, embodying both the wisdom of a sage and the ferocity of a mountain spirit, making him a compelling and enduring figure in Japanese mythology. His long nose is a defining characteristic, marking him as a Tengu, and his association with Mount Kurama reinforces his connection to the natural world and spiritual power.
subtitle: King of the Tengu, Mountain Master

keyMyths.title: Sojobo and Yoshitsune
keyMyths.description: Sojobo trains the young Minamoto no Yoshitsune in swordsmanship, transforming him into a formidable warrior. This training is crucial for Yoshitsune's later success.

keyMyths.title: Sojobo's Power on Kurama
keyMyths.description: Sojobo is said to control the weather and command the spirits of Mount Kurama. His presence ensures the mountain's spiritual power and protects it from harm.

keyMyths.title: The Wisdom of Sojobo
keyMyths.description: Seekers travel to Mount Kurama to seek Sojobo's wisdom and guidance on various matters. He offers profound insights into life, combat, and the natural world.
keyMyths.source: Legends and tales of Mount Kurama
